channel.title: Hermes Audio Overviews
channel.description: AI-generated spoken explainers from links saved and published by Hermes.
channel.itunes:author: ilteris + Hermes
channel.itunes:summary: AI-generated spoken explainers from links saved and published by Hermes.

item.title: How to Prepare for System Design Interviews
item.description: A two-host spoken overview of Hello Interview’s system design interview prep guide. The episode covers how system design interviews work, a practical delivery framework, six fundamentals to refresh, common architecture components, and how to practice using canonical problems instead of memorizing random diagrams.
item.itunes:title: How to Prepare for System Design Interviews
item.itunes:author: ilteris + Hermes
item.itunes:summary: A two-host spoken overview of Hello Interview’s system design interview prep guide. The episode covers how system design interviews work, a practical delivery framework, six fundamentals to refresh, common architecture components, and how to practice using canonical problems instead of memorizing random diagrams.

item.title: MetaSkill-Evolve: Agents Improving How They Improve
item.description: A two-host spoken explainer of MetaSkill-Evolve, a paper on recursive self-improvement for skill-based LLM agents. It explains fast task-skill evolution, slower meta-skill evolution, the five-agent improvement pipeline, persistent branch search, benchmark gains on OfficeQA, SealQA, and ALFWorld, and the main caveats.
item.itunes:title: MetaSkill-Evolve: Agents Improving How They Improve
item.itunes:author: ilteris + Hermes
item.itunes:summary: A two-host spoken explainer of MetaSkill-Evolve, a paper on recursive self-improvement for skill-based LLM agents. It explains fast task-skill evolution, slower meta-skill evolution, the five-agent improvement pipeline, persistent branch search, benchmark gains on OfficeQA, SealQA, and ALFWorld, and the main caveats.

item.title: Carmack on HBM vs Flash for AI Inference
item.description: A two-host spoken explainer unpacking John Carmack’s proposal to use cheap NAND flash as a large model-weight store for AI inference. The episode explains HBM, flash, sequential memory access, scratchpad staging, why inference is easier than training, and what this could mean for AI datacenters.
item.itunes:title: Carmack on HBM vs Flash for AI Inference
item.itunes:author: ilteris + Hermes
item.itunes:summary: A two-host spoken explainer unpacking John Carmack’s proposal to use cheap NAND flash as a large model-weight store for AI inference. The episode explains HBM, flash, sequential memory access, scratchpad staging, why inference is easier than training, and what this could mean for AI datacenters.
